Container.Name: bookorbit
Container.Overview:
BookOrbit is a self-hosted digital library and reading platform for EPUB, MOBI, AZW3, PDF, CBZ/CBR comics, and M4B/MP3 audiobooks, with sync to Kobo and KOReader devices, rich metadata lookup, reading stats, OPDS, and multi-user/OIDC support.
THIS APP REQUIRES A COMPANION DATABASE. Install the "bookorbit-db" template FIRST and let it fully start (~20-30 seconds) before starting this container.
Both containers must share a custom Docker network if they are setup to reach each other by name. Before installing, go to Docker Settings and create a custom network named "bookorbit" (Docker -> Add a new network -> name it "bookorbit", bridge driver), then attach both containers to it. Otherwise, use the database's IP and port to reach it.
Required secrets - generate these yourself before filling in the fields below: openssl rand -hex 24 -> Postgres Password (also set on bookorbit-db) openssl rand -hex 32 -> JWT Secret openssl rand -hex 16 -> Setup Bootstrap Token
After both containers are running, open App URL in your browser. You'll be redirected to a setup wizard - use your Setup Bootstrap Token to create the admin account.
Full docs: https://bookorbit.app/installation.html
Container.Requires: bookorbit-db must be created and started first and reachable by this container.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port used to reach BookOrbit. Maps to the fixed internal port 3000.

Config.Name: Books Folder
Config.Description: Host folder where your book files live. Point this at your existing media/books share.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: BookOrbit's own app data (config, generated files). BookOrbit manages ownership of this folder automatically.

Config.Name: App URL
Config.Description: Full URL you'll use to open BookOrbit, e.g. http://192.168.1.10:3000 for direct LAN access, or https://books.example.com if behind a reverse proxy.

Config.Name: Postgres User
Config.Description: Must exactly match POSTGRES_USER on the bookorbit-db container.

Config.Name: Postgres Password
Config.Description: Must exactly match POSTGRES_PASSWORD on the bookorbit-db container.

Config.Name: Postgres DB
Config.Description: Must exactly match POSTGRES_DB on the bookorbit-db container.

Config.Name: Postgres Host
Config.Description: IP of bookorbit-db container or 'bookorbit-db' if both containers share a custom network.

Config.Name: Postgres Port
Config.Description: Port the Postgres container listens on internally.

Config.Name: JWT Secret
Config.Description: Long random secret used to sign login tokens. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32

Config.Name: Setup Bootstrap Token
Config.Description: One-time token used to claim the admin account on first setup. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 16

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID BookOrbit runs/writes files as. Must have read (and write, for uploads/Book Dock) access to your Books Folder.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID BookOrbit runs/writes files as.

Config.Name: Book Dock Path
Config.Description: Advanced: internal path for the Book Dock drop-folder feature. Leave blank unless you've read the Book Dock docs and mapped an additional volume for it.

Config.Name: Fix Permissions
Config.Description: BookOrbit automatically repairs ownership of the App Data folder on startup. Set to false only if your platform manages that folder's ownership externally.

Config.Name: Client URL
Config.Description: Only needed if the frontend is served from a different domain than App URL. Leave blank to default to App URL.

Config.Name: Node Max Old Space Size
Config.Description: Node.js heap limit in MB. Raise this (e.g. 4096) for very large libraries if you see out-of-memory errors. 'auto' lets Node decide.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Log verbosity. Set to 'debug' for troubleshooting.
